questions.question: Explain why Strings are immutable in Java and how this affects case conversion operations.
keyPoints: String immutability ensures thread safety without synchronization
Immutability provides security for sensitive data like passwords
Case conversion methods return new String objects, never modify original
This design ensures consistent hashing for collections

questions.question: What is the Turkish Locale problem in case conversion? How do you solve it in Java?
keyPoints: Turkish has special rules: dotted İ/ı and dotless I/ı
Default toUpperCase('i') gives 'I' instead of 'İ' in Turkish
Use toUpperCase(Locale.TURKISH) or toUpperCase(Locale.ENGLISH)
This affects user-facing applications in Turkish-speaking regions

questions.question: Write Java code to convert the first character of a String to uppercase and rest to lowercase. Example: "jAVA" should become "Java".
keyPoints: Use substring(0,1).toUpperCase() for first character
Use substring(1).toLowerCase() for remaining characters
Concatenate both parts with + operator
Handle edge cases: empty string, single character

questions.question: What is the difference between Character.toLowerCase() and String.toLowerCase()?
keyPoints: Character.toLowerCase() operates on single char
String.toLowerCase() processes entire string
String version handles locale rules and supplementary characters
Character version returns char, String version returns new String

questions.question: Explain the output of the following code: String s = "Hello"; String t = s.toLowerCase(); System.out.println(s == t);
keyPoints: String is immutable, toLowerCase returns NEW String
Even if no conversion needed, new object may be created
'==' compares references, not content
Output is false because s and t are different objects

questions.question: Write a Java method that counts the number of vowels (case-insensitive) in a String. The method should handle null safely and use only String case conversion methods.
keyPoints: Check for null input, return 0
Convert input to lowercase using toLowerCase()
Use replaceAll() to remove non-vowels or iterate with charAt()
Count remaining characters or iterate through vowels
Consider using regex: [^aeiou] pattern to remove consonants
Return the count

questions.question: Design a Java program that converts a given sentence to title case (first letter of each word capitalized) while handling edge cases like multiple spaces and null input. Explain your approach.
keyPoints: Handle null/empty input gracefully
Split string by whitespace using split("\\s+")
For each word, capitalize first char, lowercase rest
Use StringBuilder for efficiency if processing many words
Handle edge case: single character words, all uppercase input
Return joined result

questions.question: Explain the time and space complexity of case conversion operations in Java.
keyPoints: Time complexity: O(n) - linear scan of each character
Space complexity: O(n) - new String of length n must be allocated
Character-by-character conversion with new char array creation
No in-place modification possible due to String immutability
